item.title: Refactor cmd_metrics to compute once, render twice
item.intent: Metrics are computed by one jq pass and rendered by two output modes. The 6 duplicated jq computations between JSON and human branches are eliminated. Saves ~12 lines and 6 jq forks.
item.description: cmd_metrics (114 lines) computes avg_attempts, items_per_day, by_priority, by_category, and recent-10 trends twice: once in a big jq -s block for --json output, and again as 6 separate jq -s calls for human output. Extract _compute_metrics_json that always produces the JSON object. --json mode emits it directly. Human mode extracts fields from the pre-computed JSON with jq -r.
steps: Extract _compute_metrics_json function that returns the full metrics JSON object
cmd_metrics human mode: call _compute_metrics_json, then render fields with jq -r extraction
Remove the 6 separate jq -s calls from the human branch
Verify: both --json and human output are identical to before
acceptanceCriteria: No jq -s call appears in the human-output branch of cmd_metrics
cmd_metrics --json output is identical to before
cmd_metrics human output is identical to before
